issue_title: Makeotf generates incorrect secondary lookups with useMarkFilteringSet flag
issue_body:
For the attached font and feature file, makeotf 2.0.94 generates a secondary lookup that has the useMarkFilteringSet bit set, but combines substitutions that use incompatible mark filtering sets. As a result, renderers that pay attention to this bit do not render the font as intended.

The feature file specifies three contextual lookups as part of its pres feature:
– one looking at above base marks while replacing a pre-base mark,
– one looking at below-base marks while replacing a pre-base mark,
– one looking at below-base marks and replacing one of them.

Such contextual lookups require secondary lookups in the GSUB table for the actual substitution. makeotf creates a single secondary lookup for all three primary lookups in the pres feature. It sets the useMarkFilteringSet bit, and uses the mark filtering set of the first primary lookup, so it looks at above base marks. However, the glyph that actually needs replacing in the last primary lookup is a below-base mark. Since the lookup requests filtering for above base marks, the shaping engine should skip the below-base mark and not replace it.

In reality, the current versions of the major browsers (Safari 11.0, Firefox 56.0, Chrome 61.0.3163.100, Edge 40.15063.0.0) render text as intended for the font, apparently ignoring the mark filtering set of the secondary lookup. However, native apps on Android version 6.0 or older render text incorrectly, the glyph uni102F remains visible, which indicates that the mark filtering set is respected and the final substitution is not made.

The text used for testing is U+1004 U+103A U+1039 U+100F U+103C U+102F. The intended shaping behavior is:
– reordering: uni103C uni100F uni1004 uni103A uni1039 uni102F
– rphf feature: uni103C uni100F uni1004103A1039 uni102F
– pres feature, first lookup: uni103C.tt uni100F uni1004103A1039 uni102F
– pres feature, second lookup: uni103C102F.tt uni100F uni1004103A1039 uni102F
– pres feature, third lookup: uni103C102F.tt uni100F uni1004103A1039 __.invisible

It’s not clear why makeotf sets any lookup flag at all for the secondary lookup. By the time this lookup is reached, the primary lookup has decided that the substitution should be made, so no more filtering seems necessary or appropriate. But if for some reason the filtering is needed, then substitutions with different filtering settings must be kept in separate secondary lookups.

[AFDKO bug.zip](https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/afdko/files/1351159/AFDKO.bug.zip)

issue_title: Update docs' debugging of ceph in vagrant
issue_body:
Could add a FAQ entry on HEALTH_WARN state, as it gets when using `vagrant suspend` and then `vagrant up`:

Q: `ceph -s` states HEALTH_WARN with a clock skew (see below), how do I make it right again?

Sample output:

```
root@e95e4fbd4cdd:/app# ceph -s
    cluster ff727d9d-8bbb-4308-9142-7c2364bdc8f0
     health HEALTH_WARN clock skew detected on mon.deis-03
     monmap e3: 3 mons at {deis-01=172.17.8.100:6789/0,deis-02=172.17.8.101:6789/0,deis-03=172.17.8.102:6789/0}, election epoch 30, quorum 0,1,2 deis-01,deis-02,deis-03
     mdsmap e14: 1/1/1 up {0=deis-02=up:active}, 2 up:standby
     osdmap e59: 3 osds: 3 up, 3 in
      pgmap v3271: 1536 pgs, 12 pools, 428 MB data, 652 objects
            30476 MB used, 17195 MB / 49578 MB avail
                1536 active+clean
```

A: Run this for all machines on which ceph detects a clock skew:

``` bash
sudo systemctl stop ntpd
sudo ntpd -gq
sudo systemctl start ntpd
```

This will synchronise the time over ntp, manually, and then start ntpd (the daemon) again.

The current Node and Python code rewrites street names, turning things like "W ST SEBASTIAN ST" in the source data to "West Saint Sebastian Street" in the output. The problem is the transform can only degrade information from the source. We're not using any source-specific information to add value, it's all just a guess. The current code is definitely US English only.

I think this transformation is a bad feature for the product. The Python code I'm implementing in ditch-node does it for now to make it as compatible as possible as the Node code. If we decide to remove this feature from the processing, in the Python code it's as easy as removing expand.py and any calls to it.

issue_title: Provide a way for attribution to include a source date
issue_body:
Some source licenses require attribution to include a date of the source data.
Eg Slovenia: http://www.e-prostor.gov.si/fileadmin/struktura/ANG/General_terms.pdf

> The data user shall indicate the data source in every publication of data or products. Such indications
> shall include the following data: "Surveying and Mapping Authority of the Republic of Slovenia, the
> type of data **and the time to which the data refer and/or the date of the database**."

To keep it simple for Slovenia i have reduced the "date" component of attribution to a year-only precision in [/sources/si/countrywide.json](https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/blob/master/sources/si/countrywide.json), but updates have to be done via pull requests and update from 2017 to 2018 is still waiting in https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/pull/3470 (see `sources/si/countrywide.json`)

Ideally there would be a way to extract some metadata from the source and allow that changes to attribution to be automated.

The [daily prepared souce zip file for slovenia](http://raba.openstreetmap.si/openaddresses/si-addresses-latest.zip) contains a .txt file with exact date, but that could easily be changed to provide the date in some other way, eg:
* a json with required attribution (a sort of updated local `countrywide.json` distributed with the zip file itself)
* include attribution with every row in the .csv (machine would collect them, and list all the unique attributions in the final attribution)
* include date with every row in the .csv (machine would collect them, and list all the unique attributions in the final metadata, or provide a range)
* check the timestamp of csv within the zip file

A simple, but not exact solution would be to allow some placeholder replacement within attribution text (eg to replace `{yyyy-mm-dd}` with current date and `{yyyy}` with the current year).

(ticked moved over from https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/issues/3961)

issue_title: Deleting deployment does not delete pods associated with it
issue_body:
We have the same issue with https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/32985 .

It may be the bug of `pykube` and we have tested for serval times. If we `delete` the deployment, `kubectl get deployment` can not find that deployment, but `kubectl get rs` shows that the pods are not deleted.

If we use kubectl to delete the deployment, everything works well and the pods will switch to `Terminating` and deleted forever.

We have used `pykube` to implement the PaaS service and this is critical for us. Hope anyone can help to confirm and fix this 😃

Can we simulate the given use case using ciw:
1) Say, there is a bicycle rental station where there are 100 cycles present at a time. Now the customers arrive with a certain distribution to take the cycle. Each customer is allowed one cycle at a time. The new batch of cycles comes only after 60 minutes which means that the empty cycle servers are refilled only after 60 minutes. A customer baulks if there are no cycles currently present and the refilling of the cycles will take  more than 30 minutes. The rented cycles are returned to the warehouse. There amount of resources(cycles) available in the warehouse can be taken as any number. Is there a way this can be simulated?

2) In a metro station, or even production lines, a batch of customers are served simultaneously. Like 100 people enter the metro together. Is there some way in which this can be incorporated?

issue_title: Add contextual menu handlers to Finder/explorer when running
issue_body:
Dropbox adds menu options to the Finder only when it's running. Dropbox is not present in the Dock while running and is primarily accessed through a menu item or Finder integration. Can we tap into this functionality while ipwb is running or do we need elevated access? What about Finder "Extensions" per System Preferences > Extensions?

What are some example of desired functionality that should be added to this menu?

![screen shot 2017-12-05 at 9 34 48 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2514780/33641653-69dc2946-da04-11e7-9a05-7cd4f0f40649.png)
New binary released on September 27, 2017. ipwb tests still rely on 0.4.11. This should be a simple update but we may also want to look at any new capabilities of the daemon.

Ability search to take more generic inputs, like bounding boxes, for example like:

geocode -o geojson 'iowa' | jq .bbox | planet search

issue_title: Support/examples for using TuriCreate with pandas?
issue_body:
The example in the README shows how to use Turi Create with an SFrame. However, [SFrame has not had a release since July 2016](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/SFrame).

pandas is pretty much the de-facto DataFrame library in the Python data science ecosystem. It is under [active development](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/SFrame), has a [substantial community](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/stargazers) (with [tens of thousands of StackOverflow questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pandas)), and provides [tools for many common steps in data analysis](http://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/basics.html).

Please consider aligning with the general momentum in the Python data science community by providing examples of using pandas with Turi Create.

```
class Vehicle(models.Model):
     kind = models.CharField(choices=('car', 'bike',))
     extra = hstore.DictionaryField(schema={...})
```
Is it possible to override the schema at run time so we can have different schema for cars and bikes? For example in the `__init__` of the model?

I've been tracking this repo for a while, and it seems that the activity has dropped (summer interns done?). Is there a plan for what to do with this repository now? I might be able to spend some time on it, but I'm interested to hear if there is any long term strategy planned. E.g. are there many more features that need to go in, or is this now in maintenance mode? Who will review PRs that come in? etc.

I'm asking since this offers a very nice tie-in with nbdime and the overarching goal of that task in our project (https://github.com/OpenDreamKit/OpenDreamKit/issues/95), and as such I'm invested in getting this to a workable release. I believe integration with JupyterLab/Notebook will help drive adaption and improve usability significantly.

        Button data is returned from the Esplora in negative logic format.
        This method corrects this so that a button press is a "1" and a release is "0"
        :param data: A list of sensor data elements

issue_title: Data manager does not check if index is already populated.
issue_body:
The shed-install command works really nice: you feed it a yaml and it installs the tools. Oops! You forgot a tool! No worries, just add it to the yaml and run shed-install again. It will skip the already installed tools.

This behaviour is not reproduced in the run-data-managers tool. If you run a yaml again, it will start indexing all the genomes in there. Even if they are already indexed. This is annoying if you have human genomes on the list which take a long time to index.

issue_body:

issue_title: Does the pre-trained fastText store '<' and '>' in its vocabulary?
issue_body:
According to [Enriching Word Vectors with Subword Information](https://arxiv.org/abs/1607.04606), FastText adds special boundary symbols < and > at the beginning and end of words, allowing to dis- tinguish prefixes and suffixes from other character sequences. 

For example, "where" is represented as <wh, whe, her, ere, re>, and <where>, if n=3. 

What I want to do is to use fastText to get vectors for individual character ngram. For example, I expect to get 6 vectors for "where", which will be used to do initialization. These vectors will be continued to train afterwards. I download the English fastText from [fasttext.cc](https://fasttext.cc/docs/en/pretrained-vectors.html), which is trained based on wiki.

My questions: Does fastText literaly store '<' and '>' in the vocabulary? Should I pass a char ngram with '<' and/or '>' to get the vector OR without them?

For instance, for 'where':
```
en_model = FastText.load_fasttext_format('../FastText/wiki.en/wiki.en')
# OPTION 1:
word_vector = []
for charngram in ['wh', 'whe', 'her', 'ere', 're', 'where']:
    word_vector.append(en_model[charngram])
# OPTION 2:
word_vector = []
for charngram in ['<wh', 'whe', 'her', 'ere', 're>', '<where>']:
    word_vector.append(en_model[charngram])
```

Please merge https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/pull/369 as -lost hs is broken due to commit https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/commit/4a8488df2ffd5b71f9d7123661627a1e14b02829 (missing minus sign in sigmoid function)

issue_title: How to output "allMetrics" in Cloud ML Engine hyperparamter tuning jobs?
issue_body:
The [docs describe](https://cloud.google.com/ml-engine/reference/rest/v1/projects.jobs#hyperparameteroutput) an `allMetrics` field in the hyperparameter tuning output which contains TensorFlow output values such as loss, accuracy, precision, recall, etc. I found [this](https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/c07bd5a12e561ebe4e074b218bdd29304cb7a370/google/cloud/ml/v1/job_service.proto#L379) in the repo as well. My output for each tuning trial contains `finalMetric` which in this case is loss, since that is what I configured the hyperparameter trainer to optimize for:
```
"finalMetric": {
    "trainingStep": "12345",
    "objectiveValue": 0.12345
}
```
But it does not contain `allMetrics`, and it is not clear how to make it output any values in `allMetrics`. Is this something I can put in my configuration file or pass as a command line argument when submitting the training job? Any suggestions would be appreciated.

issue_title: Help incorrectly indicates you can specify domains without a flag
issue_body:
Current help:

`  letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [domains]`

We should remove the domains part, since they are specified with -d or --domains.

We should have a new `renew` verb which sets defaults appropriate to renewal, tries to get information from renewal configurations when possible, and actually performs renewals.

The `renew` verb is akin to calling `certonly` for each existing renewal configuration file.

issue_title: Nginx restoring older backups than it should
issue_body:
Bug #921 keeps coming back to plague me. When I first ran Nginx, I had an /etc/nginx/conf.d/ssl.conf with the directive:

`ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m;`

However, as documented in #921, that causes a conflict with Nginx's own configuration. We should fix this conflict, but this bug is about another issue: My ssl.conf keeps getting overwritten with that original version.

I edited /etc/nginx/conf.d/ssl.conf to comment out the ssl_session_cache line, and next time I run the letsencrypt client, the line becomes uncommented.

It seems like I have a copy of ssl.conf stuck in /var/lib/letsencrypt/IN_PROGRESS/ssl.conf_2, and on each run that copy gets written to /etc/nginx/conf.d/ssl.conf before the restart for DVSNI. That causes a failure to restart, and the challenge fails.

interesting thing is, that on another computer with same configuration the client work fine.
OS and installed packages:

OS Centos 6.7
Python 2.6.6
letshelp-letsencrypt, letsencrypt and acme in versions 0.1.1 (installed from pypi)
executed bootstrap/centos.sh from latest letsencrypt git tree
cli.ini:
rsa-key-size  = 2048
email         = mymail.mydomain.tld
server        = https://acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
account       = some_number
config-dir    = /mnt/data/letsencrypt
work-dir      = /mnt/data/letsencrypt/lib
logs-dir      = /mnt/data/letsencrypt/log
text          = True
agree-tos     = True

I'm on Ubuntu 14.04.

`./letsencrypt-auto --apache -c cli.ini -d heimkoma.com` gives the following error:

I'm having to create this on a separate server that doesn't have the SSL virtualhost for heimkoma.com

```
Failed authorization procedure. heimkoma.com (tls-sni-01): unauthorized :: The client lacks sufficient authorization :: Correct zName not found for TLS SNI challenge

IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - The following 'unauthorized' errors were reported by the server:

   Domains: heimkoma.com
   Error: The client lacks sufficient authorization

   To fix these errors, please make sure that your domain name was
   entered correctly and the DNS A record(s) for that domain
   contain(s) the right IP address.
```

`cli.ini` contains the following:

```
# This is an example of the kind of things you can do in a configuration file.
# All flags used by the client can be configured here. Run Let's Encrypt with
# "--help" to learn more about the available options.

# Use a 4096 bit RSA key instead of 2048
rsa-key-size = 4096

# Always use the staging/testing server
server = https://acme-v01.api.letsencrypt.org/directory

# Uncomment and update to register with the specified e-mail address
email = foo@example.com

# Uncomment to use a text interface instead of ncurses
# text = True

# Uncomment to use the standalone authenticator on port 443
# authenticator = standalone
# standalone-supported-challenges = tls-sni-01

# Uncomment to use the webroot authenticator. Replace webroot-path with the
# path to the public_html / webroot folder being served by your web server.
# authenticator = webroot
# webroot-path = /usr/share/nginx/html
```

`letsencrypt certonly` modifies the paths passed as `--cert-path` and / or `--fullchain-path` before saving the certificates. This means the resulting paths are unpredictable (without knowledge of `letsencrypt` internals, that is). Quite bad for automation.

In case there are no existing files at the given paths, `letsencrypt certonly` should simply use the paths as-is. The default action in case there are existing files is up for debate, but there should be way to tell `letsencrypt certonly` to overwrite the existing files ("batch mode") if that's not the default.

issue_title: MultiAuthentication with BasicAuthentication and Authentication does not work as expected
issue_body:
I would like to combine Authentication and BasicAuthentication with MultiAuthentication, like this:

authentication = MultiAuthentication(BasicAuthentication(),Authentication(),)

When not using basic authentication the API would return objects that are visible to the anonymous user. When using basic authentication the API would return objects visible to a specific user plus the ones visible to the anonymous user.
Unluckily when trying to access the API using Firefox with a url such as:
http://user:password@localhost:8000/myapp/api/v2.4/mymodel/?format=json&limit=10

Firefox complains that 
"You are about to log in to the site "localhost" with the username "opweb", but the website does not require authentication. This may be an attempt to trick you." 

any idea what could be wrong here?

Note that using curl everything works perfectly, but from the browser and from an external application I am getting this behavior.

issue_title: builder 'images' does not have a data_type
issue_body:
### Steps to Reproduce

I created an hdf5-based .nwb file. It has multi-electrode electrophys data in it, with both LFP and spike trains. I can't read the file I created. This is super depressing.

Download .nwb file from here (~820MB):

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1W0w-7VtHWXv9LEIlYw7meInxvF2sYpv0/view?usp=sharing

Run this code:

```
io = NWBHDF5IO('/path/to/downloaded file')
nwb = io.read()
```

Get this error:

```
ValueError: builder 'images' does not have a data_type
```

### Environment
Please describe your environment according to the following bullet points.
- **Python Executable:** Conda 5.2
- **Python Version:** Python 3.6
- **Operating System:** Ubuntu

issue_title: 在测试 Session 部分使用 Mock
issue_body:
<!--
Issue tracker 只用于反馈 bug 和 feature request 。
与 WeRoBot 用法相关的问题请加入主页上的 QQ 群讨论或在 SegmentFault 社区提问。
在提问前请仔细阅读我们的文档，并推荐阅读《提问的智慧》：
https://github.com/ruby-china/How-To-Ask-Questions-The-Smart-Way/blob/master/README-zh_CN.md
-->

* **遇到的问题**
<!-- feature 总是要解决问题，来讲讲遇到的问题吧 -->
CI 装各种 Database 太慢了。

* **解决方案**
<!-- 描述为了解决这个问题需要 WeRoBot 添加的 feature -->
使用 Sqlite Mock 全家！——whtsky

* **其他内容**
<!-- 如相关 issue 或 PR 的引用等信息 -->

## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

The python client was not architected inline with the other clients. For example, adding a new HTTP sender will require an extensive surgery and a lot of technical debt to maintain backwards compatibility; in the other clients, adding a new HTTP sender was a breeze.

There are other tasks we can accomplish such as:

- Support opentracing 2.0.0 #199 (Work already [underway](https://github.com/uber-common/opentracing-python-instrumentation/pull/54)).
- Reduce the public API to prevent semver issues #185 
- Remove the `enabled` config variable #145
- Remove tornado dependency #31 

## Proposal - what do you suggest to solve the problem or improve the existing situation?

Here's a list of changes, I'll try to make this as exhaustive as possible but I may be missing some changes:

- Create a new `Sender` interface ala the [golang client](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/blob/master/transport.go) which supports just `append` and `flush` as begun [here](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/fbefb070c6dce91e757fecebbe8add760fe5d620/jaeger_client/senders.py). `LocalAgentSender` should be removed entirely and assimilated into `UDPSender` (or made a private class). `Reporter` should accept a `Sender` instead of `LocalAgentSender`.
- `LocalAgentHTTP(host, sampling_port, throttling_port)` -> `RemoteConfigManager(host, config_port)`. Since sampling_port and throttling_port is always the same, we can unify them. Update `RemoteThrottler` and `RemoteControlledSampler` to accept `RemoteConfigManager` instead of `LocalAgentSender`.
-  `config.py` changes
  - Update `throttler`
    - Remove `port`
  - Remove `sampling_refresh_interval`
  - Update `sampler`
    - Add `refresh_interval`
  - Remove `enabled` #145
  - Add `remote_config`
    - Add `host`
    - Add `port`
  - Remove `reporter_batch_size`
  - Remove `reporter_flush_interval`
  - Remove `logging`
  - Rename `local_agent` -> `reporter`
    - Remove `enabled`
    - Rename `reporting_host` -> `host`
    - Remove `reporting_port` -> `port`
    - Add `batch_size`
    - Add `flush_interval`
    - Add `log_spans`
- Remove all deprecated functions such as `span.error` and `span.info`
- Reduce the scope of the API and make private certain fields. Fields TBD.
- Remove tornado dependency.

I'll keep adding to this list, probably missing something.

## Any open questions to address

- Internally, the bare minimum config we use to enable tracing is `enabled: true`, not sure what the impact of removing will be.
- I've checked internally and these breaking changes should have 0 impact on our customers, cannot say the same for external users.

issue_title: Crash in `BlenderModifier_Subdivision :: DoIt` when importing a blender mesh

issue_body:
The Cinema4D support is completely outdated. I'm no expert on the tool or file format, and no contributor either, so I'd like to know
1) if anyone here uses it at all
2) if anyone is still maintaining it
3) if we can update it to support the latest SDK.
Details:
It doesn't compile with the current Melange SDK (v16, the C4D importer was written for v6), but this can be fixed easily.
Once it compiles, there are strange ABI incompatibilities to the Melange SDK. Specifically, the type information seems incompatible -- causing dynamic_cast to fail, debugger showing wrong types. I "fixed" it by overriding type checks.
Obviously, this is no stable solution, that's why I'd like to get in touch with an expert.
Regards

issue_title: SMD Import Texture Garbage at end of String
issue_body:
Hi there, there seems to be an issue with the SMD importer pertaining to texture strings.

When calling GetTexture:

pMaterial->GetTexture(aiTextureType_DIFFUSE, 0, &Path, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL) == AI_SUCCESS

The path seems to have some sort of garbage at the end of the string and for some reason won't load a texture unless I copy it to an std::string and do stringName.pop_back(). I think it may be an issue with not NULL terminating the string or something to that effect.

When I try to print the name to the console with anything else that I'm printing with it, the name kind of gets overwritten.

Anyway, it seems like a simple fix, but I just can't read some of the C style code you got to understand what's going on. If I figure it out, I'll do a pull request.

If you need an example model and texture, here you go:

https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B0a1deqARl2MRmlTd1lTUGNpVnM/view?usp=sharing

It would be nice if ASSIMP could provide an option to calculate (or better approximate) the surface curvature per vertex. Slangly spoken this is just the angle between neighbour triangles. There are different approaches to approximate the curvature of the continuous surface that is represented by the mesh. If someone is interested in implementing this, I would like to help test different approaches.

As a dpp user, I want to be able to pre-define unnecessary columns for my data, so that after running `dpp run xxx`  output data does not have those columns 

## Acceptance Criteria

* [x] able to delete column with `dpp`

## Tasks 

* [x] write spec
* [x] write tests
* [x] add `remove_fields` processor to common processors
* [x] update README

## Analysis 

Spec: 

```
run: delete_fields
parameters:
  resources: resource_name
  columns:
    - column_name1
    - column_name2
```
bypassing workers is not working as mentioned in the readme docs : https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/psq#bypassing-workers-for-testing

So I'm using psq in a light-demand environment, and am encountering abysmal latency on the workers pulling jobs off the queue. As in 10 minutes when things are "speedy", and up to 25 minutes in some cases. I see that the code depends on the behaviour of google.cloud.pubsub.Subscription.pull() in blocking mode. Would modifying the psq code to do its own polling on the unblocked pull() improve that, or would the underlying subscription service still be taking its own sweet time to deliver messages even if we were banging on it every 10 seconds? I can dig into this, but thought you might already have some insights on this problem.

issue_title: Connection reset by peer in logging client libs
issue_body:
I use gcloud logging and datastore client libs on my GCE VMs. Sometimes (rather sporadically) my jobs fail due to connection reset by peer. The only common pattern is that it happens in long running jobs.

The stacktrace goes something like this:

```python
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gcloud/logging/logger.py", line 189, in log_text
    client.logging_api.write_entries([entry_resource])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gcloud/logging/connection.py", line 150, in write_entries
    data=data)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gcloud/connection.py", line 343, in api_request
    target_object=_target_object)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gcloud/connection.py", line 241, in _make_request
    return self._do_request(method, url, headers, data, target_object)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/gcloud/connection.py", line 270, in _do_request
    body=data)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/oauth2client/transport.py", line 169, in new_request
    redirections, connection_type)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1609, in request
    (response, content) = self._request(conn, authority, uri, request_uri, method, body, headers, redirections, cachekey)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1351, in _request
    (response, content) = self._conn_request(conn, request_uri, method, body, headers)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1307, in _conn_request
    response = conn.getresponse()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/httplib.py", line 1073, in getresponse
    response.begin()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/httplib.py", line 415, in begin
    version, status, reason = self._read_status()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/httplib.py", line 371, in _read_status
    line = self.fp.readline(_MAXLINE + 1)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/socket.py", line 476, in readline
    data = self._sock.recv(self._rbufsize)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/ssl.py", line 714, in recv
    return self.read(buflen)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/ssl.py", line 608, in read
    v = self._sslobj.read(len or 1024)
error: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer
```

I'm not totally sure, but this could be because of the connection pool being maintained by the underlying `httplib2` library. `httplib2` is holding connection pools, but doesn't seem to be refreshing them. So, it could be that it holds a connection which has become stale (if no request happened in a while), and thus server sends a connection reset.

I suspect some of my datastore requests are also failing due to a similar issue.

Since in my use-case I have a cron job that runs once a day and consumes the PubSub I need a way to know when the topic is empty (another process feeds the topic once a day) and stop the job once that happens, the behaviour we had in version 0.27.0 (blocking read) was much friendlier and if I ever needed to do something else while wait I could do that with python `multiprocessing`.

issue_title: New test cases that will currently fail
issue_body:
I have a handful of cases found in the wild that are not currently supported, or in some cases give incorrect results.  In each case I have a fix ready, but some of them required non-trivial refactoring.  I'll make PRs for any that you want to support.

```
dstr = "File Creation Time: 0513201421:33"
parse(dstr, fuzzy=True)
# desired: datetime(2014, 5, 13, 21, 33)
# actual: ValueError: hour must be in 0..23

dstr = "1991041310:19:24"
parse(dstr, fuzzy=True)
# desired: datetime(1991, 4, 13, 10, 19, 24)
# actual: ValueError: hour must be in 0..23

# Infer day/year from the trailing "th"
dstr = "06 June 11th"
parse(dstr, fuzzy=True)
# desired: datetime(2006, 6, 11)
# actual: datetime.datetime(2011, 6, 6, 0, 0)

# The "94" is getting identified as a year at the beginning, then
# it balks at the "1994"
dstr = "SMITH R &  WEISS D 94 CHILD TR FBO M W SMITH UDT 12/1/1994"
parse(dstr, fuzzy=True)
# desired: datetime(1994, 12, 1)
# actual: ValueError: Unknown string format


# The 2011 is identified as the year, and so the "2012" is slotted into
# the un-filled hour/minute
dstr = "2011 MARTIN CHILDREN'S IRREVOCABLE TRUST u/a/d NOVEMBER 7, 2012"
parse(dstr, fuzzy=True)
# desired: datetime(2012, 11, 7)
# actual: datetime(2011, 11, 7, 20, 12)
```

issue_title: Bug in formatting mathjax/LaTeX inconsistently depending on chosen delimiters, markdown interaction
issue_body:
I just discovered that the notebook formats the same LaTeX input in different ways depending upon your choice of equation delimiter for LaTeX/mathjax which traditionally should not matter.

An example of this bug (plus a png of the correctly rendered incorrect rendering, since github apparently has its own bugs regarding displaying LaTeX/mathjax embedded in notebooks) can be found at https://gist.github.com/michaelpacer/2b627ffe8f10cde53926.

Put briefly, it's not supposed to matter whether you choose to delimit LaTeX formatted math with `\(…\)` and `$…$` for inline equations or `\[…\]` or `$$…$$` for displayed equations. Of course, because `\` is used as an escape character we have to instead use`\\(…\\)` `\\[…\\]`, which in theory is fine. However, because LaTeX formatted subscripts use the `_` symbol, and that conflicts with markdown's interpretation of `_…_` as an indicator of italics, it seems that notebook parses these two different ways of delimiting equations differently. If you use `\\(…\\)` `\\[…\\]` then you need to escape underscores `\_` but that means that your LaTeX is now invalid outside of the notebook (as can be seen in the rendering of `\_` when using `$…$` and `$$…$$` as the delimiters). 

For now it seems safer to guide people to just use `$…$` and `$$…$$`, since then at least it will interpret valid LaTeX properly (this has the unfortunate side-effect of conflicting with one of the [LaTeX "deadly sins"](http://ctan.mirrorcatalogs.com/info/l2tabu/english/l2tabuen.pdf), but I think that's a less big issue than writing inaccurate LaTeX to get accurate formatting, imho). 

I'm not sure if there is going to be an easy or even possible fix for this (since I know this kind of parsing is incredibly difficult to manage) but I was befuddled with why my properly formatted LaTeX was not being rendered at all, so I figured I'd bring it to people's attention.

As per the [Rackspace official docs](https://support.rackspace.com/how-to/installing-python-novaclient-on-linux-and-mac-os/) I ran the following in a Python 2.7 virtualenv:

```
$ pip install rackspace-novaclient
$ nova image-list
```

This yielded an error:

```
ERROR (ImportError): No module named openstack.common
```

It turns out that the latest version of `python-novaclient` is `4.0.0` and because `rackspace-novaclient` does not place any version contraints on the `python-novaclient` dependency, the latest version is what is installed.

It seems like this one of several issues with more recent versions of `python-novaclient` (#16 mentions a problem with version 3.x).

The `python-novaclient` dependency in `setup.py` should define a "known good" [semantic version](http://semver.org/) range. This would allow the `rackspace-novaclient` maintainers to ensure compatibility with later `python-novaclient` versions before they are included with `rackspace-novaclient`.

issue_title: Adding built-in resilience into jira library
issue_body:
@ssbarnea , I was thinking... We are facing a lot of issues in the test suite, which are linked to the fact that JIRA Cloud is not always responsive, and does not finish the job internally by the time the next bit of code is run (#366, #373, #374 and counting)... Well isn't that something that people will face in their own usage of the library as well?! 

I mean, a lot of code is written in the tests to check, wait and make sure that we can go through the next bit of code. Why not instead build that in the library? Like having an `ensure_transaction` flag, which if set to `True`, would check that the action has taken effect. 

It could take this form:
```
    def add_group(self, groupname, ensure_transaction=False, max_tries=5, wait_time_before_retry=1):
```
In the above example, if `ensure_transaction` was set to `True`, then a `find()` would be triggered after the `add_group` action is sent to JIRA, and if it cannot find the group, it would try up to `max_tries` times and wait `wait_time_before_retry` seconds between each try.

We could do the same for the deletes on Resources, amongst other actions:
```
    def delete(self, params=None, ensure_transaction=False, max_tries=5, wait_time_before_retry=1):
```

What do you think ?

issue_title: Documentation error can cause individual to delete S3 data unknowingly.
issue_body:
The [documentation here](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/guide/migrations3.html#access-controls) reads:

```
# Boto 2.x
bucket.set_acl('public-read')
key.set_acl('public-read')

# Boto 3
bucket.Acl().put(ACL='public-read')
obj.put(ACL='public-read')
```

Which, at least to me, leads to believing that the equivalent of `bucket.Acl().put(...)` for keys is `obj.put(...)`, however this appears not to update the ACL but instead replaces the object at that key with an empty file whose ACL is 'public-read'. What should be there is, I think:

`obj.Acl().put(...)`

issue_title: Changing session profile doesn't change credentials
issue_body:
A simple test:

    In [1]: import botocore.session
    In [2]: session = botocore.session.get_session()
    In [4]: session.profile = 'cdn'
    In [5]: session.get_credentials().access_key
    Out[5]: 'AKIATHISISNOTMYACCESSKEY'
    In [7]: session.profile = 'personal'
    In [8]: session.get_credentials().access_key
    Out[8]: 'AKIATHISISNOTMYACCESSKEY'

Basically, the credentials never change regardless of what the profile is set to.  This seems wrong to me. If not, we should probably remove the property setter on the profile attribute.

issue_title: Support for customize the max connections and max pools
issue_body:
When Boto is used to perform multiple requests concurrently, for example to sped up the write throughput for DynamoDB, it uses the default parameters given by the `HTTPAdapter` [1], that is currently set to 10 by default.

This number means all of those amount of connections over this number will be dropped and they will not be recycled after, therefore when a new connection is necessary - and the first 10 are bussy - a full handshake TCP trip is performed, that will add a important ``latency`` for the whole time operation.

The idea will be add the properly fields to customize the current max connections and max pools, giving to the user the freedom to set this values to other ones different than the default ones.

Does it make sense for you  ?  if it does, I can try to send the properly pull request with a tentative solution.  


[1] https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/vendored/requests/adapters.py#L82

> A potentially interesting side-effect of having dfk.<wait/join> is that we could switch defer task launches until wait it called, at which point the dfk can do resource planning on the available DAG and then do smarter launches. We would be able to borrow some cool features from systems that do static planning on workflow DAGs

And @benclifford:
> That second bit of behaviour doesn't necessarily have to be in a global-style wait: let your code generate app futures as it cares to. Don't schedule them. As soon as you block by asking for any result of a future, schedule everything you need for that future and its predecessors (or schedule everything). That might be less deadlock prone than holding off scheduling until a user makes an explicit call.

Logs for the user to understand what apps ran, how, where, and what happened.
Logs for the dev for complete sequence of events.

issue_title: Allow adding to, not just replacing into metadata using function_addons.py
issue_body:
As implemented, metadata_utilities.merge_dicts() ignores (read: does not merge) keys in the dictionary that don't already exist in the dictionary being merged into.

In practical terms, this means that our code-generated function metadata must have all keys that we care about.

For example: functions.py marks *every* function as 'codegen_method': 'public'.
Then we go and replace some of those with 'private' or 'no'.

This is silly, the generator should decide those defaults, rather than doing it by hardcoding it in the functions.py metadata.

This is a bigger problem as we add more metadata. For example, as part of the solution to https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/issues/205 I want function metadata to specify for each function if it is used in the context of error reporting. But as it is, if metadata contains info only for the small subset where the value is True, it doesn't get merged because the key didn't exist in the first place in the source metadata.

Method:

    set_sequence(self, values, source_delays=None)

corresponds to function:

    ViStatus _VI_FUNC niDCPower_SetSequence(
        ViSession vi,
        ViConstString channelName,
        const ViReal64 values[],
        const ViReal64 sourceDelays[],
        ViUInt32 size);
 
The way the function works, sourceDelays array is an optional parameter. If you pass NULL (in Python the default is None), then the driver uses whatever default for sourceDelay it has. This doesn't work in our generated code:

    TypeError: object of type 'NoneType' has no len()

So the default doesn't work. That's bad. It gets worse...

Suppose the Python client does pass two lists, but they are of different size. The generated code will grab the size from one of the two, and pass that to the Library. If the one it grabbed it from happens to be larger, then you will end up passing a bunch of garbage from the smaller one (or perhaps zeroes, if ctypes zeroes out array element on allocation).

There are a few system tests, but it's hard to verify that all the right values make it down correctly.

* test_set_sequence_default_source_delays()
* test_set_sequence_no_source_delays()
* test_set_sequence_with_source_delays()
* test_set_sequence_with_too_many_source_delays()
* test_set_sequence_with_too_few_source_delays()

with switch_session.connect(channel1, channel2):
    # do some nidmm measurements

When you exit, the connection is automatically opened, even if you exited with an error.

issue_title: Question about finding all possible solution of simple equation using Z3 in Windows 10, VS2013
[UBSan] call to function through pointer to incorrect function type when running C++ API examples
issue_body:
When building Z3 with UBSan, when the C++ api examples are executed the following error is reported.

```
/home/user/z3_src/src/api/api_context.cpp:278:13: runtime error: call to function (unknown) through pointer to incorrect function type 'void (*)(_Z3_context *, Z3_error_code)'
(/home/user/z3_build/examples/cpp_example_build_dir/cpp_example+0x4ad480): note: (unknown) defined here
```

This is for 110d558ee4262721f3ab2c999d789eaf84ac15d6

For this input:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_BV)
(declare-fun s0 () (_ BitVec 32))
(define-fun s1 () (_ BitVec 32) #x0000000a)
(define-fun s2 () Bool (bvugt s0 s1))
(check-sat-assuming (s2 (not s2)))
(get-unsat-assumptions)
```

I'm getting:

```
(error "line 6 column 24: invalid check-sat command, argument must be a Boolean literal")
unsupported
; get-unsat-assumptions line: 7 position: 22
```

I do believe the input format is correct for `check-sat-assuming`, so I'm curious why z3 is saying the arguments are not boolean.

Also, am I to conclude `get-unsat-assumptions` isn't implemented yet? That seems rather unfortunate given there seems to be support for `check-sat-assuming`.

[problem.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/1803696/problem.zip)

The attached model inappropriately returns sat. Editing the code and specifying the value of one variable (e.g. v3^2 = 0) from the model that is returned causes the solver to return unsat.

Tested with both 4.6 and recent commit 58544925048b8559880edb20ccca385c895f1acb

_>> Follows from bug report #815, it reports a different inconsistent result within the same general set of formulas_ 

On the attached _lexicographic problem_ ([formulas.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/630942/formulas.zip)), both **wmax** and **maxres** return the following optimum combination:

**f_maxres.smt2/f_wmax.smt2:**
```
sat
(objectives
 (weight.Auto 506)
 (workTime.Auto 23)
 (cost.Auto 336)
)
```

However, if one expands _assert-soft into ite_ statements _[a necessary step, because assert-soft ids are not terms in z3]_ and checks the satisfiability of the returned solution by adding

```
;; Check optimum value
(assert (and
 (= weight.Auto 506)
 (= workTime.Auto 23)
 (= cost.Auto 336)
))
```

to the expanded formula, then **z3** returns _unsat_:

**e_maxres.smt2/e_wmax.smt2**:

```
unsat
```

Previous versions of **z3+maxres**, returned the correct optimal solution on the same formula:

```
sat
(objectives
 (weight.Auto 509)
 (workTime.Auto 23)
 (cost.Auto 336)
)
```

The version of **z3** tested in this bug report corresponds to commit f1a704484b56555311b52dad2ab4146a5577f257 .

issue_title: Control whether to use incremental or "bulk" SAT solver
issue_body:
Apparently, Z3 has multiple SAT solvers. There's an incremental one that is more efficient for push/pop scenarios and there is a "bulk" solver that is more efficient to solve a problem just once.

It is not quite clear to me which one is picked under what circumstances. What exactly triggers what solver to be used?

Can we control the solver to be used? That would be useful because we could empirically test which one performs better for the particular problems that are posed. If this is not possible right now I would like to request this as a feature.

issue_title: Unsat core returns constraints it shouldn't return
issue_body:
Consider the following script:
`(set-option :produce-unsat-cores true)
(define-sort A () (Array (_ BitVec 16) (_ BitVec 8)))


(declare-fun M1_670_1 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun M1_670_4 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun M1_689_0 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun MAC1_667 () (_ BitVec 16))

(declare-fun M1 () A)

(assert (! (distinct (bvand #x01 (bvsub M1_689_0 M1_670_1)) #x00) :named p1))
(assert (! (= M1_670_1 (select M1 MAC1_667)):named p2))
(assert (! (= M1_670_4 (select M1 MAC1_667)):named p3))
(assert (! (= M1_689_0 (select M1 MAC1_667)):named p4))


(check-sat)
(get-unsat-core)`

It returns p1 p2 p3 p4, while the true core is p1 p2 p4

issue_title: Can the short timeout when calling Pyboard.follow() through ampy run command be removed?
issue_body:
When `Pyboard.exec_raw()` calls `Pyboard.follow()` and a `timeout=10` is defaulted, this only gives a short time (I assume around 10 seconds) before the `ampy run` command without `--no-output` will raise `PyboardError('timeout waiting for first EOF reception')`. This is a problem if you have a long running program from which you wish to see output in a terminal window (I'm running on Windows 10) when it completes (you just get the pyboard error message).
I have modded `def exec_raw(self, command, timeout=10, data_consumer=None):` in `pyboard.py` to have a default `timeout=None` which allows `ampy run` to continue until the program running on the pyboard (in my case an ESP32) completes.
Is this a reasonable change that can be made or will it affect other operations?
Implement changes necessary to communicate with https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSHandoverService for mail_draft and handover commands.

issue_title: Unable to debug C# Functions in VS2017 Preview after clean install
issue_body:
### Description
After having to repave my machine, I tried to re-install my Azure Functions tooling. The order of the install was:

- VS2017 Preview (15.3)
- Node.js/NPM
- Azure Functions Extension for VS2017 Preview
- Azure Functions Core Tools (via NPM)

Tried to debug a C# Function but I got the following error:
![functionerror](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4126750/27604845-8a062548-5b72-11e7-86be-3a5aa9cee7f0.png)

I then installed the **Azure Function CLI (old version)** which failed to install due to bandwidth issues. To fix this I download it manually following the steps outlined here: [https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Functions/issues/110](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Functions/issues/110). 

I unzipped the files in the following location:
`C:\Users\<userprofile>\AppData\Local\Azure.Functions.Cli\1.0.0-beta.9`

This made VS2017 to stop complaining, but the Function failed to run since there was a mismatch between the CLI tools and the latest Functions project format (WebJobs based). 

###Workaround
To make it work, I went in my NPM package folder and copied the executable and accompanying dlls to the directory that VS2017 expects to find them. To make it more explicit, I copied from here:

**C:\Users\<userprofile>\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\azure-functions-core-tools\bin**
to
**C:\Users\<userprofile>\AppData\Local\Azure.Functions.Cli\1.0.0-beta.9**

With this workaround, I was able to run and debug the latest Functions code in VS2017
---

### Environment summary

**Install Method:** How did you install the CLI? (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, nightly)  
Answer here:
Azure Functions Core tools ->NPM 
Azure CLI -> manually

**CLI Version:** What version of the CLI and modules are installed? (Use `az --version`)  
Answer here:
Azure Functions Core Tools (1.0.0-beta.99)
Function Runtime Version: 1.0.11002.0

**OS Version:** What OS and version are you using?  
Answer here: Windows 10 Creator's update

As a first time command author and neophyte Python user, below are the bumps I hit along the way when following the readme:
* lack of explanation and examples around the azure.cli.core.commands.parameters (tags_type, location_type, enum_choice_list, etc.)
* how completion lists work and when to use them (such as resource_name_completion_list)
* how completers work in general and when to use them, although I think this is a more general python gap for me, it would be nice to understand why and when we would use argcomplete.completers to enable certain customer scenarios (for example, FilesCompleter, what does it mean to include this in a command?)

issue_title: Option to disable automatic saving of authentication token
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When you use az login an authentication token is saved that is available for 14 days. So you only need to log in every 14 days. So if your laptop is compromised they will have access to your Azure account. If the last log in you did was with a user that for example has owner rights, things could get nasty.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
As far as I can tell there is not way to disable this. For Azure Powershell there is Disable-AzureRmContextAutosave. I would be very nice to have such an option for the Azure CLI.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The best solution would be that you always had to use az login, and there was an option to enable the token to live for 14 days.

Having to add `--location` for `az create` can be defaulted to either a global setting or by subscription. 

related https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/2662

issue_title: azure network vpn-gateway create giving an unclear error
Getting error 'unicode' object has no attribute 'get'
issue_body:
When I try to run this command: https://github.com/atrauzzi/anecdote-azure/blob/master/bin/deploy-functions#L68-L76 , I end up getting this error:

```
'unicode' object has no attribute 'get'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/azure/cli/main.py", line 37, in main
    cmd_result = APPLICATION.execute(args)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/azure/cli/core/application.py", line 157, in execute
    result = expanded_arg.func(params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/azure/cli/core/commands/arm.py", line 267, in handler
    instance = getter(client, **getterargs) if client else getter(**getterargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/azure/mgmt/resource/resources/operations/resources_operations.py", line 520, in get
    exp = CloudError(response)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/msrestazure/azure_exceptions.py", line 136, in __init__
    self._build_error_data(response)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/msrestazure/azure_exceptions.py", line 156, in _build_error_data
    data = data.get('error', data)
AttributeError: 'unicode' object has no attribute 'get'
```

There is small but growing divide the between capability of AZ CLI and Azure Powershell.

Of particular note is object creation in Powershell. In Powershell, a complete configuration object can be created using multiple commands. [Example ($gw)](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/application-gateway-configure-redirect-powershell):
```
# Get the application gateway
$gw = Get-AzureRmApplicationGateway -Name AdatumAppGateway -ResourceGroupName AdatumAppGatewayRG

# Get the existing HTTPS listener
$httpslistener = Get-AzureRmApplicationGatewayHttpListener -Name appgatewayhttplistener -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Get the existing front end IP configuration
$fipconfig = Get-AzureRmApplicationGatewayFrontendIPConfig -Name appgatewayfrontendip -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Add a new front end port to support HTTP traffic
Add-AzureRmApplicationGatewayFrontendPort -Name appGatewayFrontendPort2  -Port 80 -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Get the recently created port
$fp = Get-AzureRmApplicationGatewayFrontendPort -Name appGatewayFrontendPort2 -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Create a new HTTP listener using the port created earlier
Add-AzureRmApplicationGatewayHttpListener -Name appgatewayhttplistener2  -Protocol Http -FrontendPort $fp -FrontendIPConfiguration $fipconfig -ApplicationGateway $gw 

# Get the new listener
$listener = Get-AzureRmApplicationGatewayHttpListener -Name appgatewayhttplistener2 -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Add a redirection configuration using a permanent redirect and targeting the existing listener
Add-AzureRmApplicationGatewayRedirectConfiguration -Name redirectHttptoHttps -RedirectType Permanent -TargetListener $httpslistener -IncludePath $true -IncludeQueryString $true -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Get the redirect configuration
$redirectconfig = Get-AzureRmApplicationGatewayRedirectConfiguration -Name redirectHttptoHttps -ApplicationGateway $gw


# Add a new rule to handle the redirect and use the new listener
Add-AzureRmApplicationGatewayRequestRoutingRule -Name rule02 -RuleType Basic -HttpListener $listener -RedirectConfiguration $redirectconfig -ApplicationGateway $gw

# Update the application gateway
Set-AzureRmApplicationGateway -ApplicationGateway $gw 
```

**Why does this matter/needed?**

Application Gateway can take a whole day(!) to deploy due to each individual change taking anywhere from [5-45 minutes to apply](https://feedback.azure.com/forums/281804-azure-resource-manager/suggestions/19119910-application-gateway-management-operations-are-agon).

Powershell avoids this by generating the configuration object and then applying it in bulk to the Application Gateway, resulting in only 1 operation.

Presumably this will continue to be the case with more and more PaaS offerings in Azure. Is the right answer here to build this capability in AZ CLI or perhaps the right answer is to improve mgmt PaaS offerings that are so cludgy? This would be a suggestion for the former, as to some extent understand why Application Gateway takes some time to apply changes (multiple nodes, etc).

issue_title: requests-ntlm sends POST/PUT data three times
issue_body:
Problem:
HttpNtlmAuth sends data three times for POST and PUT.

The flow is following:
- a program sends a POST/PUT request
- before the request is send, HttpNtlmAuth prepares the request, sets a hook on response
- the whole request is sent, data included
- server returns 401
- HttpNtlmAuth recognizes 401 and does retry_using_http_NTLM_auth
- sends the data again
- sends the data again, this time 200 is expected

This is quite fragile and slow, especially when you try to send a huge request - hundreds of MBs to a webdav server.

issue_title: Deal with legacy deploy_excludes intransitive mode support
issue_body: Currently we support a global legacy mode via `JvmBinaryTasks`'s `--shallow-deploy-excludes`.  This support helps out Square for one who relies on the shallow deploy_exclude behavior to cherry-pick jars out of a binary deployjar and then place them elsewhere in a custom bundle with a custom task.  The option doesn't make alot of sense as a global one though in the general case, it would make more sense if the intransitive bit could be written down in `JvmBinary` targets.  Either support this level of customization or deprecate and remove the global option once users have an alternate path forward.

issue_title: NodeSet.hpp uses a macro that it doesn't include
Provide source install for C++ dependencies
issue_body:
related to #593 

Currently most dependencies are prebuilt and the binaries are included in the nupic.core repo. This requires us to rebuild the libraries for each platform which can be error prone and bloat the repo. It also means that most platforms simply aren't supported.

This task is proposing that we use CMake ExternalProject functionality to fetch a specific source version of each C++ dependency from external repos. This should allow nupic.core to be built on a broad range of platforms and it will make it easier to maintain dependencies and update versions. We can add a caching layer to our CI system to reduce build times if that is problematic.

- [x] Cap'n Proto #626
- [ ] [Provide source install for apr dependency](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/987)
- [ ] [Provide source install for apr-util dependency](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/988)
- [ ] [Provide source install for z dependency](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/989)
- [ ] [Provide source install for yaml dependency](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/990)

`utils.get_value()` is the entry-point for most of the fields accessor methods.

Its interface (aka. arguments order on a call) is different from a generic and common interface of a getter, be that for a `dict` or an `object`. This means that you can not overwrite this method with a simple assignment of `Field.get_attribute = dict.get` or `Field.get_attribute = getattr`. Instead you would need to create a new method that properly maps the arguments. This needs to be fixed... hopefully in 3.0.

This started here:
https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/commit/7cba47481cbfb0f7e8b3fd36b303a419e236812a#commitcomment-14486377

Lets keep this task to remind us fixing it in the next major release.

```

When you pass string constant into coalesce, it will be expanded as identifier instead of string constant:
`db().select(db.table.str_field.coalesce('foo'))`
would expand into this:
`SELECT COALESCE(table.str_field,foo) FROM table`
but the expected behavior is this:
`SELECT COALESCE(table.str_field,'foo') FROM table`

html5lib .999

issue_title: calculation error : NUMEXPR 2.4.4, Python 3.4.3, Pandas 0.17 or 0.16.x, Windows 7/10 64bit
issue_body:
When numexpr is available to pandas the first two rows of a pandas dataframe have incorrect values. When I remove numexpr availability to Pandas the values are computed correctly.

Operation 1 (copy) data, values are correct
Operation 2 (add) values in first two rows are set to a small values (e.g.) 2.4-312.

df['new']=df['a'].copy()
df['new']=+10.0 

Using numpy directly produces correct result.
Using pandas without numexpr produces correct result.
Using pandas with numexpr produces erroneous results.

I see the same type of result for multiplication. It only affects the first two rows in dataframe.


===VERIFIED 2.4.3 works!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

100 test runs

The issue with high probability is with numexpr 2.4.4

I would be willing to work on this.  The C++ standard library does not have great support for Bessel functions, but the boost library has a good implementation [here](http://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_62_0/libs/math/doc/html/math_toolkit/bessel/bessel_first.html).  What is this project's policy on including pieces of external libraries like boost?

issue_title: Clarify naming of Language independent folders
issue_body:
There's a lack of clarity on this feature in 2.x & master, mostly (IMO) because of the variety of names it has been given, including:
* Neutral root folder (title of the section in Readme.rst)
* language neutral folder (also mentioned in Readme.rst)
* language independent folder (the name of the FTI type, only visible in ZMI & code I think)
* lif (abbreviation of the above, which unfortunately clashes with "Language Independent Field", but fortunately only visible in code)
* Language shared (name of the folder according to Readme.rst)
* Media (*real* name of the folder in 2.x)
* "shared folder" (how it is referenced in translate menu)

So may I humbly suggest, 
1. the primary stakeholders decide on **one** name to use going forward?
2. can we change Readme.rst accordingly.

@bloodbare @saily @datakurre @sneridagh @jensens and anyone else?

It's not my place (as a minor contributor) to select which name should be used, but I've put a rewrite of the "Neutral root folder" section in Readme.rst as how I think best supposing that you selected "language neutral folder".   (Note I'm currently using 2.x, so tweaks *might* be required to the below for master (though I think not).  

> ## The "Media" folder - a shared "language neutral folder"

> The root language folders are used to house the content tree for the corresponding language.  However, there are some use cases where we need content that does not belong to any language. For example, for assets or side resources like images, videos and documents. For this reason PAM supplies a special **language neutral folder** to house these kind of objects. After PAM setup, there is a special folder called "Media", which can be accessed through the "Go to shared folder" item of the "Translate" menu. All items placed in this folder will have neutral as their default language and will be visible from the other root language folders as if they were placed there as well.  
**Note:** *Language Neutral Folder*'s have also been historically known as "Neutral root folder", "language independent folder" and "language shared (folder)"

issue_title: timedeltaformat tracebacks with the latest Babel release (0.9.6)
issue_body:
The latest official Babel release (0.9.6 at the time of writing) does not have format_timedelta() function in the dates module. Using the timedeltaformat filter in a template causes a traceback.

The missing function is available in the development version of Babel, though, and will be included in the 1.0 release, according to the  [ChangeLog](http://svn.edgewall.org/repos/babel/trunk/ChangeLog). With the installation of Flask-Babel (easy_install or pip), one gets the latest released version of Babel and will not be able to use the timedeltaformat filter.

issue_title: global: flask-oauthlib prevents reading the request stream
issue_body:
For every API request we call [``verify_oauth_token_and_set_current_user``](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauth2server/blob/master/invenio_oauth2server/ext.py#L129). This method calls Flask-Oauthlib which makes a call to [``request.form.to_dict()``](https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/blob/master/flask_oauthlib/utils.py#L31)

Accessing ``request.form`` makes Werkzeug parse the request body, and thereby preventing e.g. Invenio-Files-REST to read the request body via ``request.stream``, which is needed for fast file uploads.

issue_body:
**Problem:**
In B2Share code we were using invenio-records-rest with a few different endpoints (POST creates a deposit instead of a Record). Because of that we were using invenio-records-rest as a library and we disabled the automatic creation of endpoints (`RECORDS_REST_ENDPOINTS={}` in config). We reused the View classes and replaced the only method which was blocking us, i.e `RecordsListResource.post`. This does not work anymore as The views code depend explicitly on the config variable `RECORDS_REST_ENDPOINTS` (see [ext.py](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/f0b44bc8b64ea01c13dbd0bc5736df81c8f9752e/invenio_records_rest/ext.py#L82) and [views.py](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/f0b44bc8b64ea01c13dbd0bc5736df81c8f9752e/invenio_records_rest/views.py#L451))

From what I understood at the time this was implemented in B2Share, using invenio-records-rest as a library was an accepted use-case so I mark this as a bug.

Blocks #99

issue_title: Client should gracefully handle connection resets
issue_body:
When a server due to inactivity closes its connection, a socket error is raised when the client tries to use the connection - this is expected behaviour, however the crate client removes the given server from its active server lists as if it was down, which is wrong. This leads to no remaining servers exceptions if only one server is used in the connection. In conjunction with #302 this leads to sporadic exceptions which could be handled gracefully on the http level.

issue_title: Executable normalization breaks custom entry points
issue_body:
The executable normalization methods introduced in 82369fd appear to have broken our custom entry point, resulting in unexpected "Executable not found" errors.

Contents of our `.pre-commit-config.yaml` file:

    - repo: git://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    sha: v0.4.2
    hooks:
    - id: trailing-whitespace
    - id: debug-statements
    - id: flake8
        name: Flake8 on commit diff
        description: This hook limits Flake8 checks to changed lines of code.
        entry: git diff HEAD^ | flake8 --diff

When using version 0.7.6, the latter custom hook does precisely what we want: only changed lines of code are checked for PyCodeStyle violations.

But subsequent 0.8.x versions produce the following error:

    Executable `git diff HEAD^ | flake8 --diff` not found

Is there another way we should be handling things in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` to account for the aforementioned executable parsing changes made in 0.8.x? Or something else we should be doing to achieve the desired result?

If not, could perhaps the entry point / executable parsing logic be modified such that only the first "word" is analyzed, with the rest ignored? (In this case, `git` would be found in $PATH, so no error would result.)

Or perhaps a configuration setting that skips the executable parsing logic would be the best solution?

issue_title: Show error when installing dependency fails?
issue_body:
A package that I included in my `Pipfile` failed to install when I ran `pipenv install`, so pipenv failed with the message
```
pip.exceptions.InstallationError: Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in <source directory>
```
Would it be a good idea for pipenv to show more information when the install fails (possibly behind the `--verbose` flag)?

(By the way, I'm getting this error with a local dependency—not sure if that makes a difference.)

issue_title: Pipenv does not lock dependencies of installed wheels

When installing a dependency through a wheel (using either `https://` or `file://`), the initial installation will work fine and install all the library's dependencies into your virtual env. Running `pipenv graph` will show the dependency tree correctly. The resulting `Pipenv.lock` file though, will not have the child dependencies of the wheel (even after running `pipenv lock` again).

Hi guys.

The tools shows below that work with the default firmware of Zigbee Wireless:

zbid – List available devices
zbfind – GUI for Zigbee location tracking – Never got this working 
zbopenear - ZigBee/802.15.4 many channel listener (need 1 RZUSB per channel)
zbwireshark - Sends sniffed ZigBee packets to Wireshark via a named pipe
zbdump – tcpdump clone (libpcap or commercial Daintree SNA format)
zbconvert – Convert capture file formats (libpcap -> Daintree)
zbdsniff – Scans capture files for Zigbee encryption keys (takes a capture file)
zbgoodfind – Search a binary file to identify encryption key for encrypted .


The tools that require the killerbee firmware:
zbreplay – Replay network traffic from libpcap or Daintree files
zbscapy – scapy for ZigBee
zbkey – Attempts to retrieve a key by fake association and request/response
zbassocflood – Transmit a flood of associate requests to a target network
Killerbee comes pre-installed with most Kali distributions, however your success will vary.  Here was our out-of-the-box experience:

the first problem I have faced is:
Exception: Unable to open device.  Ensure the device is free and plugged-in

and the second: 
usb.core.USBError: [Errno 110] Operation timed out !!


The setup I was used is:

# mkdir tools; cd tools
# git clone https://github.com/riverloopsec/killerbee.git
# apt-get install libgcrypt-dev
# cd killerbee
# python setup.py install

# sudo rm -rf /usr/lib/pymodules/python2.7/killerbee
# python setup.py install
----------------------
Unfortunately, when I tried to type "Zbid" or other command -- I got this wrong message !!
 [IpmportError No module named killerbee]

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0518/

`pyproject.toml`, if it exists, should be used like `setup_requires`.

See also https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3691

According to [RFC 7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3), "the product identifiers are listed in decreasing order of their significance for identifying user agent software."

Setuptools instead supplies a user agent like "Python-urllib/3.5 setuptools/21.2.2".

Better would be for setuptools to follow the RFC, placing 'setuptools' first.

issue_title: Will not overwrite existing env if old python is removed
issue_body:
The way to "upgrade" a virtualenv when a new underlying python version is installed seems to be to recreate the virtualenv over the top of the old one. That has been the case for some years and I think has been working for me although I can't be 100% certain that I've done a python upgrade in that time, but I find it unlikely I wouldn't have.

Today I tried to upgrade a virtualenv for this exact reason and I now get an error.

```
$ mkvirtualenv django-cookiecutter
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/virtualenv", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 825, in main
    symlink=options.symlink)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 985, in create_environment
    site_packages=site_packages, clear=clear, symlink=symlink))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 1179, in install_python
    copyfile(join(stdlib_dir, fn), join(lib_dir, fn), symlink)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 479, in copyfile
    copyfileordir(src, dest, symlink)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 454, in copyfileordir
    shutil.copytree(src, dest, symlink)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.10/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/shutil.py", line 177, in copytree
    os.makedirs(dst)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.10/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 157, in makedirs
    mkdir(name, mode)
OSError: [Errno 17] File exists: 'django-cookiecutter/lib/python2.7/config'
```

I tried on several virtualenvs (some had --system-site-packges, some did not) and it is the same in every case.
I think it is basically because when I upgraded python to 2.7.10, 2.7.9 was removed. This created a broken symlink for lib/python2.7/config and that causes the failure.

I thought this was a virtualenvwrapper issue so I filed it here: https://bitbucket.org/dhellmann/virtualenvwrapper/issue/268/mkvirtualenv-will-not-overwrite-existing, but the maintainer let me know it was a virtualenv problem.

Cheers

issue_title: Error parsing VCF format line 'AD' when 'Number=R'
issue_body:
Hi, thanks for pysam! 

There's an issue when parsing the vcf format line:
`##FORMAT=<ID=AD,Number=R,Type=Integer,Description="Number of observation for each allele">`

VCF version 4.2 uses the symbol `R` for representing the fields that have one value for each possible allele (including the reference).
This should include the field `AD` ("Number of observation for each allele") but pysam (versions `0.13` and `0.11.2.2` on OSX) throws an error:
```
ValueError: Formatting error in the format string in line 70: '##FORMAT=<ID=AD,Number=R,Type=Integer,Description="Number of observation for each allele">'
Error BADLY_FORMATTED_FORMAT_STRING: Formatting error in the format string
```

issue_title: preparing for pysam unittests fails in tests/pysam_data
issue_body:
I'm updating to pysam-0.14 (yay it builds thanks to all the work from the earlier build problems on 0.13). I'm using the [Github tarball](https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/archive/v0.14.tar.gz).

When running the following to prepare the tests, I get this error in pysam_data:
~~~
make all -C tests/cbcf_data
make all -C tests/pysam_data
pytest-2.7 tests
~~~
This fails when Making inside pysam_data:
~~~
samtools faidx ex1.fa.gz
cp ex1.bam ex1_csi.bam
samtools index -c ex1_csi.bam
make: *** No rule to make target `example_reverse_complement.bam', needed by `all'.  Stop.
~~~

If I remove the attempt to create `example_reverse_complement.bam` in `tests/pysam_data/Makefile`, then the tests finish:
~~~
895 passed, 15 skipped, 1 warnings in 152.92 seconds
~~~

Is the `example_reverse_complement.bam` a leftover line from earlier tests?

It would appear that the attribute `AlignmentFile.is_bam` is only reflecting wether the `b` flag used to open a given SAM file, instead of testing the content of the file and ascertaining if it is actually a BAM.  As an example:

```
>>> sam = pysam.AlignmentFile("example.sam", 'rb')
>>> sam.is_bam
True
>>> sam = pysam.AlignmentFile("example.sam", 'r')
>>> sam.is_bam
False
```

Since BAM files are built with a standard [BZGF header](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gzip#File_format), would it not make sense to test the file for the GZIP magic string `0x1F8B` at the start of the file?

issue_body:
Currently, the "usual" way to run an `ApplicationSession`-derived component is via the `ApplicationRunner` API. This lumps together several things:
- creates the component (ComponentConfig + instantiating the session)
- possibly sets up TLS options, if `wss://` URI
- starts the event-loop (`reactor.run()` for Twisted or `run_until_complete` in asyncio).
- starts logging (via `txaio.start_logging`).
- handles several error-cases

This becomes awkward for certain use-cases, and e.g. spawned a `start_reactor=bool` kwarg for the Twisted version (which also lumps in logging, because `start_logging` is only called if it starts the reactor itself).
- users wanting to start their own logging (e.g. https://github.com/crossbario/txaio/issues/75#issuecomment-216651341)
- users wanting to run the event-loop elsewhere (e.g. `start_reactor=` kwarg)
- users who want to handle errors themselves

I am filing this bug to collect these use-cases as a way to improve the "I wish to run a component" API to ensure they're covered.

issue_title: During deployment, new instances raise PartitionsOwned error.
issue_body:
We utilize a balanced consumer with rdkafka, running inside a docker container orchestrated by AWS ECS. We run two consumer instances in a group consuming a topic of 2 partitions. I have noticed a trend during deployment, in which the new instances raise PartitionsOwned error. The deployment process involves spinning up new container instances, and deprecating old containers, and switching over connections. This leads me to believe that, for a moment, kafka perceives that 4 consumers are trying to consume 2 partitions, and as kafka law dictates, those newest consumers will receive no partitions. My question is, does pykafka provide an interface to explicitly deregister a consumer. I am hoping to de-register consumer upon a sigint, as provided by container stop. 

Below is how we instantiate consumer, roughly...

```
ConsumerClass(object):
	def __init__(self, consumer_group, auto_commit_enable=True, auto_commit_interval=1000, managed=False, use_rdkafka=False):
	    self.consumer = self.topic.get_balanced_consumer(
	        consumer_group=consumer_group.encode("utf-8"),
	        auto_commit_enable=auto_commit_enable,
	        auto_commit_interval_ms=auth_commit_interval,
	        managed=managed,
	        use_rdkafka=use_rdkafka)
```
We use rdkafka==0.9.1,
Python==3.6
Pykafka==2.5.0

Also, fyi we utilize python tornado in this service, but that doesn't really affect pykafka as the consumer runs on its own thread(s).

How would you suggest de-registering a consumer, is the library handling this ? I did not see in library code where this happens...

issue_title: Race condition causes use-after-free when sending
issue_body: spead2::send::stream::send_next_packet allocates the next packet on the stack, then asynchronously sends it. This means that the memory dynamically allocated for holding the header and item pointers can be freed before they are transmitted if the socket would block.

issue_title: Extend exporter API to return source of project files instead of writing them to disk
issue_body:
The exporters in mbed are undergoing a huge revision and part of that revision is a zero-copy strategy. The goal is to create a zip file from an mbed project, using progen, and enforce that only the final zip file is written to disk. Currently, the only files that have two copies (one in the zip, one in the build directory) are:
 - `mbed_config.h` (not progen's problem)
 - project files, generated by progen

The feature request is this: I would like an API from the exporters that allows me to generate the project files without generating them to a file.
